message:
fix(escape-parser): tmux_cc feature requires std (ft-t995m)

## Defect

frankenterm/escape-parser is `#![cfg_attr(not(feature = "std"), no_std)]`
(src/lib.rs:41). The tmux_cc feature enables a pest-generated parser
plus the tmux_cc/mod.rs module, both of which depend on std-only
items — HashMap, String, ToString, format!, to_string(), and so on.

The Cargo.toml tmux_cc feature entry pulled in pest + pest_derive
but did NOT chain `std`:

  tmux_cc = ["dep:pest", "dep:pest_derive"]

So `cargo {check,test} -p frankenterm-escape-parser --features tmux_cc`
produced 90+ `E0425 / E0433 / E0599` errors like:

  method not found in `&'static str`
  ... items from traits can only be used if the trait is in scope;
  help: `use crate::alloc::string::ToString;`

Every test site behind `mod tests` inside tmux_cc/mod.rs (including
`parser_events_are_chunk_boundary_invariant`) inherited the breakage.
The tmux_cc feature build was effectively dead in CI — ft-kfy99's
(tmux-line-cap) regression test and every other tmux_cc test could
only run under the docs feature bundle, which incidentally pulled std
in via use_serde/std.

## Fix

Chain `std` into the tmux_cc feature declaration:

  tmux_cc = ["dep:pest", "dep:pest_derive", "std"]

One line in Cargo.toml, plus an inline comment cross-referencing
ft-t995m so the next reader sees why std is load-bearing. Matches
the shape the `docs` feature bundle uses implicitly (docs → use_serde
→ ... → std).

## Verification

Pre-fix:
  $ cargo check -p frankenterm-escape-parser --tests --features tmux_cc
  ... 90 errors, build fails ...

Post-fix (this commit):
  $ cargo check -p frankenterm-escape-parser --tests --features tmux_cc
    Finished `dev` profile [unoptimized + debuginfo] target(s) in 3.86s

Clean build. All test modules under tmux_cc now compile, unblocking
the ft-kfy99 regression test plus
`parser_events_are_chunk_boundary_invariant` plus every future
proptest shipped behind the feature.

## Scope note

No downstream behavior change: the default feature set for this
crate doesn't include tmux_cc, and the docs bundle already pulled
std transitively. The fix just makes the single-feature case
self-contained.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -54,7 +54,15 @@ kitty-shm = ["dep:nix", "dep:winapi"]
 use_serde = ["dep:serde", "frankenterm-color-types/use_serde", "frankenterm-blob-leases/serde", "bitflags/serde", "frankenterm-input-types/serde"]
 use_image = ["image", "dep:image"]
 image = ["sha2", "frankenterm-blob-leases"]
-tmux_cc = ["dep:pest", "dep:pest_derive"]
+# [ft-t995m] tmux_cc requires std — the generated pest parser and the
+# module's own code both rely on HashMap/String/ToString/format!,
+# none of which are available when the crate compiles with the
+# default `no_std` shape. Without the `std` implication, building
+# with `--features tmux_cc` alone produces 90+ "trait not in scope"
+# errors. Declaring the dependency makes `cargo test --features
+# tmux_cc` work out of the box and matches the shape the docs
+# feature (which already pulls tmux_cc + std via use_serde) uses.
+tmux_cc = ["dep:pest", "dep:pest_derive", "std"]
 docs = ["use_serde", "image", "tmux_cc", "kitty-shm"]
 
 [dev-dependencies]
